pgxxvi-p1-s2: These men may well have a remedy for thousands of such situations.
pgxxvi-p1: growth inherent in this group they may mark a new epoch in the annals of alcoholism. These men may well have a remedy for thousands of such situations.
pgxxvi-p2-s1: You may rely absolutely on anything they say about themselves.
pgxxvi-p2: You may rely absolutely on anything they say about themselves.
pgxxvi-p3: Very truly yours, William D. Silkworth, M.D.
pgxxvi-p4-s1:
The physician who, at our request, gave us this letter, has been kind enough to enlarge upon his views in another
statement which follows.
pgxxvi-p4-s2:
In this statement he confirms what we who have suffered alcoholic torture must believe-that the
body of the alcoholic is quite as abnormal as his mind.
pgxxvi-p4-s3:
It did not satisfy us to be told that we could not control our
drinking just because we were maladjusted to life, that we were in full flight from reality, or were outright mental
defectives.
pgxxvi-p4-s4: These things were true to some extent, in fact, to a considerable extent with some of us.
pgxxvi-p4-s5:
But we are sure
that our bodies were sickened as well.
pgxxvi-p4-s6:
In our belief, any picture of the alcoholic which leaves out this physical factor
is incomplete.
pgxxvi-p4:
The physician who, at our request, gave us this letter, has been kind enough to enlarge upon his views in another
statement which follows. In this statement he confirms what we who have suffered alcoholic torture must believe-that the
body of the alcoholic is quite as abnormal as his mind. It did not satisfy us to be told that we could not control our
drinking just because we were maladjusted to life, that we were in full flight from reality, or were outright mental
defectives. These things were true to some extent, in fact, to a considerable extent with some of us. But we are sure
that our bodies were sickened as well. In our belief, any picture of the alcoholic which leaves out this physical factor
is incomplete.
pgxxvi-p5-s1: The doctor's theory that we have an allergy to alcohol interests us.
pgxxvi-p5-s2:
As laymen, our opinion as to its soundness may, of
course, mean little.
pgxxvi-p5-s3: But as ex-problem drinkers, we can say that his explanation makes good sense.
pgxxvi-p5-s4:
It explains many
things for which we cannot otherwise account.
pgxxvi-p5:
The doctor's theory that we have an allergy to alcohol interests us. As laymen, our opinion as to its soundness may, of
course, mean little. But as ex-problem drinkers, we can say that his explanation makes good sense. It explains many
things for which we cannot otherwise account.
pgxxvi-p6-s1:
Though we work out our solution on the spiritual as well as an altruistic plane, we favor hospitalization for the
alcoholic who is very jittery or befogged.
pgxxvi-p6-s2:
More often than not, it is imperative that a man's brain be cleared before he
is approached, as he has then a bet-
